content.full_text:
Re:
F arn P olrrrcAI- P RACrrcEs C ouvrrs s roN
P.O. Box 807 c 428 J Street o Sacramento. CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
October 11,2001
Cathy Watson
Office of the Secretary for Education
ll21 L Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, CA 95814
Your Request for fnformal Assistance
Our File No. l-01-197
Dear Ms. Watson
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Because your question does not concern a specific
governmental decision, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.' This
letter should not be construed as advice on any conduct that may have already taken
place. Finally, our response is based on the facts presented. The Commission does not
act as a finder of fact in its advice-giving capacity . (In re Oglesby (197 5) 1 FppC ops.
7t)
QUESTION
Does ownership of shares of stock in Edison International represent a violation of
the state's conflict of interest laws?
CONCLUSION
No. Nothing in the Act prohibits the ownership of stock in any company
1 Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations 4ppear at Title 2, sections
18109-18-997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (g 83114; Reg. 18329, subd. (cX3).)
ffiJ


File No. I-01-197
Page No. 2
FACTS
You write regarding the issue of owning energy stocks while holding a position in
the Office of the Secretary for Education ("OSE"), which is an appointed position. You
currently own 100 shares of Edison International. These shares of stock were a gift from
your father in April of 2000 You have never actually purchased shares of stock and do
not have any plans to do so.
Your position at the OSE is as a legislative coordinator. You track all educational
legislation for your office and maintain and update your databases on the current status of
the legislation. You do not have any duties that require you to participate in energy
decisions in any way.
ANALYSIS
The Political Reform Act nowhere prohibits ownership of any given stock or
other investment interest. Rather, the Act states in Government Code section 87100:
"No public official at any level of state or local government shall
make, participate in making or in any way attempt to use his official
position to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or
has reason to know he has a financial interest."
Thus, the mere fact alone, of ownership3 of Edison International stock does not give rise
to a conflict of interest for you.
Instead, potential conflicts of interest arise where there is a confluence of an
economic interest with a governmental decision. To implement section 87100, the
Commission has adopted an eight-step analysis to determine whether a public official
may have a conflict of interest. (Reg. 18700, copy enclosed.)a One of the elements in
determining the presence or absence of a conflict of interest is the identification of a
governmental decision in which the official will make, participate in or influence.
(Regs. 18700 - 18708.) Because you have not identified any future or pending decision
in which you may participate, we cannot advise whether you may or may not have a
conflict of interest atthattime by virtue of your ownership of Edison International stock
and the specific performance of your duties.
3 An investment irfierest worth $2,000 triggers a potential conflict of interest. ($$ 82034,
87103, subd. (a).)
o Step One asks whether the individual is a public official. Step Two asks whether the official
will be making participating in making or using or attempting to use his or her position to influence a
decision. Step Three identifies the public official's economic interests. Step Four determines whetler the
given interest identified in Step Three is direcfly or indirectly involved in the governmental decision. Steps
Five and Six apply ttre materiality standard appropriate for the given type of economic interest at issue and
deternrines whether it is reasonably foreseeable that the governmental decision r,vill have a material
financial effect on the economic interest. (Reg. 18700.) Steps Seven and Eight are exceptions to the
conflicts provisions, to be considered in the event a conflict ofinterest arises.


File No. I-01-l9l
Page No. 3
You are encouraged to write the Commission for further advice when and if more
specific facts arise. In the meantime, I have enclosed afact sheet designed to help public
officials determine whether they may have a conflict of interest.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660
Sincerely,
Luisa
C.S
Tocher
Counsel, Legal Division
CSrjg

Enclosures
